_title: South African Higher Education — Funding, Accommodation & Spending (national)
_note: National figures from official sources. Per-university splits of NSFAS / self-funded / external funding are NOT published consistently, so funding-mix and accommodation figures are given at national level. Per-university total enrolment is in each university's info.json (students).

studentFunding.nsfasBeneficiariesProvisional2024Note: Provisionally funded as at 1 March 2024 (universities + TVET colleges)
studentFunding.nsfasSpend2024Note: Almost R50 billion budgeted for the 2024 academic year
studentFunding.nsfasSinceInception: More than 5 million beneficiaries funded since inception

missingMiddle.note: NSFAS Loan Scheme for the 'missing middle' — SA citizens/permanent residents above the R350k NSFAS bursary threshold

fundingCategories.note: Fully subsidised — tuition, accommodation, allowances; the largest funded group
Company bursaries, SETAs, foundations, university merit awards, international scholarships
Students/families paying fees directly — the remainder not covered by NSFAS or bursaries

studentAccommodation.privateAccommodationNote: Provision of private student accommodation is largely unregulated; NSFAS accredits and grades private providers for funded students via the registration portal (nsfas.org.za).
studentAccommodation.note: NSFAS-funded students may only be placed in NSFAS-accredited accommodation; the accreditation backlog and the broader bed shortfall remain major access barriers.

higherEducationSpending.note: DHET (Vote: Higher Education) covers university subsidies, NSFAS transfers, TVET & CET colleges, SETAs and the National Skills Fund.

perUniversity._note: Per-university NSFAS beneficiary counts are NOT published in a consolidated, current form. Counts vary enormously between institutions (e.g. TUT ~30,000 vs Stellenbosch ~5,000), so extrapolating from a national rate is unreliable and is NOT done here. Only individually-sourced figures are listed; the remaining universities do not publish a comparable figure. Each university's total enrolment is in its info.json (students).

benchmarks.minimumShare: Parliament: no university funds fewer than 50% of its students via NSFAS — but research-intensive universities are lower in practice (Stellenbosch ~5,000 of ~33,000 ≈ 15%).
benchmarks.concentration: The majority of NSFAS beneficiaries are at universities (vs TVET colleges); the largest numbers are at Gauteng institutions.
benchmarks.approved2026: 1.24 million+ students approved for 2026 funding; R6.3 billion disbursed early 2026; ~660,000 cleared and ~190,000 did not meet the academic criteria.

verified.name: Tshwane University of Technology
verified.note: ~30,000 students assisted with more than R1.4 billion in financial aid annually

verified.name: Stellenbosch University
verified.note: ~5,000 registered NSFAS-funded students (subject to change during the funding cycle)
